intro: Monteverdia tetragona, commonly known as Tetragonolobus, is a plant species belonging to the family Celastraceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the reduction of inflammation, antimicrobial activity, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential use in treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of M. tetragona include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its pharmacological properties.
